summary.currentCityDistinctNote: Only AVG_LOW/AVG_HIGH bind to city. Other 2 Q&A pairs are 100% hardcoded plaintext appearing identically on 740/740 pages.
summary.dataGapSummary: Only 1 of 6 proposed FAQs requires new data (state-level HVAC permit/license data). 5/6 wire to existing slots in data/hvac-city-context.json, data/city-context.json, data/city-cost-multipliers.json.

currentFAQs.q: How much does a new HVAC system cost in {{CITY}}?
currentFAQs.a: Most homeowners in {{CITY}} pay between {{AVG_LOW}} and {{AVG_HIGH}} for HVAC replacement, depending on system type and efficiency.
currentFAQs.citySignal: weak — cost range only, generic explanation

currentFAQs.q: Should I replace my AC and furnace at the same time?
currentFAQs.a: Replacing both at once is usually more cost-effective than separate replacements. Matched systems run more efficiently and most contractors offer bundle pricing.

currentFAQs.q: What SEER rating should I choose?
currentFAQs.a: 14 SEER is the minimum federal requirement. 16 SEER offers a good balance of efficiency and cost. 18-20+ SEER provides the best energy savings but costs significantly more upfront.

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: How much does HVAC replacement cost in {{CITY}}, {{STATE}}?
proposedFAQs.aTemplate: Most homeowners in {{CITY}} pay between {{AVG_LOW}} and {{AVG_HIGH}} for a full HVAC replacement. {{COST_DRIVER_SENTENCE_1}}

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: Why does HVAC cost more|less in {{CITY}} than the national average?
proposedFAQs.qSelector: above|below national avg by serviceMultipliers.hvac sign
proposedFAQs.expectedDistinctness: very high (this is a brand-new FAQ filled with the costDriverNote which is currently in the data file but renders nowhere)

slots.name: CLIMATE_NOTE
slots.note: Already rendered in 'what locals should know' cards but currently NOT in FAQ. Reusing in FAQ is fine — the body section is still climate-aware via systemTip.

slots.name: SEER_RECOMMEND_BY_ZONE
slots.source: data/city-context.json#climateZone -> small map (hot_humid→18+, hot_dry→16-18, mixed→16, cold→14-16)

proposedFAQs.expectedDistinctness: medium-high (climateNote is per-city; SEER map gives 4-5 buckets)

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: When is the best time to install HVAC in {{CITY}}?
proposedFAQs.expectedDistinctness: high (98% per-city unique seasonNote already exists)
proposedFAQs.note: Like CLIMATE_NOTE, this is currently in the local-context cards. Reuse in FAQ is acceptable — same text appearing in two sections of the same page doesn't hurt uniqueness BETWEEN pages, which is what Google measures.

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: What HVAC contractor red flags should I watch for in {{CITY}}?
proposedFAQs.expectedDistinctness: very high (currently unused data; pure city-specific)

proposedFAQs.qTemplate: Do I need a permit for HVAC installation in {{CITY}}, {{STATE}}?

slots.name: CITY_PERMIT_OFFICE_NOTE
slots.source: data/city-context.json#permitNote (currently roofing-worded; adapt or leave generic)
slots.note: Existing permitNote text says e.g. 'The City of Auburn requires building permits for roof replacements'. For HVAC FAQ we'd want HVAC-specific phrasing. Two options: (a) drop the city portion and rely on state intro alone; (b) add an hvacPermitNote slot to hvac-city-context.json (one-time bulk script).

proposedFAQs.expectedDistinctness: medium (state-level signal × 50, plus city-level if option-b)
proposedFAQs.deferrable: yes — without Phase 2 data fill, ship 5 FAQs. Permit FAQ alone is the only one needing new data.

unusedRichSlotsCurrentlyDormant.currentUseSite: NONE — populated but never rendered anywhere
NONE — populated but never rendered anywhere

fields: hvacPermitIntro — 1-2 sentence per-state intro to mechanical permits (varies by state code adoption — IBC/IMC year; some states delegate to county/municipal, others have state mechanical board)
mechanicalLicensingBody — e.g. CSLB (CA), TDLR (TX), DLLR (MD), DPOR (VA), CCB (OR), DOPL (UT)
salesTaxOnLabor — yes/no/some (drives total cost differences city-distinct from labor multiplier)
phaseTwoDataNeeded.researchSource: ICC code adoption table + state contractor board websites + state DOR sales tax FAQ. One-shot ~4 hours research, no API spend.
phaseTwoDataNeeded.deferrable: yes — without it, we ship 5 city-aware FAQs instead of 6. Worth doing because the 50-state file is reusable by plumbing, electrical, and any vertical needing permit/licensing data.

phaseThreeApproach.templateChange: Replace 3 hardcoded <details class="faq-item"> blocks in templates/hvac-city-page-template.html with {{HVAC_FAQ_BLOCK}} placeholder.
phaseThreeApproach.builderChange: Add buildHvacFAQ(city, state, ctx, stateData) to scripts/build-hvac-pages.js — mirrors existing buildHvacLocalContext() pattern at scripts/build-hvac-pages.js:21.
phaseThreeApproach.outputContract: Returns 5 or 6 <details class="faq-item"> blocks with city-specific content in answer text. Pure HTML, no runtime JS.

estimatedLift.expectedAfterPhase3: FAQ composite 75-85% (matches Tier A profile), full-body composite 65-72% (FAQ is ~80 words of 410; lift bounded by what other sections contribute).
estimatedLift.confidence: high for FAQ slice (data is already 98-100% unique), medium for full-body (depends on whether body sections also get a depth pass in Phase 4)
